scene_title: Ministério da Paz — mesa de guerra
narrative_beat: Medium overhead of war table with rich bronze and lead tank miniatures, golden plaque MINISTÉRIO DA PAZ, silhouetted generals

style_reference.textual_description: dark cinematic chiaroscuro, Caravaggio-inspired, foreground in rich warm tones (crimson, amber, gold) with background in desaturated black-and-white, film grain, candlelight, floating orange embers

subject.primary: a long oak war planning table with bronze and lead miniature tanks, soldiers and artillery arranged on a dark map
subject.focal_point: a heavy golden engraved plaque at the table's near edge reading MINISTÉRIO DA PAZ
subject.secondary: Silhouetted uniformed generals hunched over the map in P&B at the far end.

environment.location: the war room of the Ministry of Peace

composition.foreground: miniatures and plaque in rich bronze, crimson and gold

lighting.rim_light: gold rim on miniatures and plaque

style.post_processing: halation, crushed blacks, amber highlights, anamorphic lens flares
style.texture_detail: 35mm film grain, dust particles, floating orange embers

symbolism.secondary: miniatures as rehearsal for real death

negative_as_positive: clean frame without visible text or watermarks
photorealistic documentary cinematography, not illustrated or cartoon
analog film photography aesthetic, no digital art flatness
muted desaturated background palette, warm saturated foreground only

in_scene_text.font_style: bold engraved serif capital letters in warm polished gold
in_scene_text.medium: engraved on a heavy rectangular brass plaque
in_scene_text.placement: lower center of frame on the table edge

multimodal_inputs.instruction: Transfer color palette, lighting, grain, atmosphere only. Do not copy composition.
